content.problemStatement: Write a C program that calculates and prints the sum of the squares of all numbers from 1 to a given positive integer n using a for loop.
constraints: All values fit in 64‑bit signed integer
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single positive integer N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the sum of squares of all integers from 1 to N.

sampleTestCases.explanation: T=1. Sum of squares from 1 to 10 equals 385.

solution.approach: The sum of squares of the first N natural numbers has a well‑known closed form: 1²+2²+…+N² = N·(N+1)·(2N+1)/6. Using this formula we can compute the answer for each test case in constant time without any loops. The formula fits in a 64‑bit signed integer for the given limits, so we can safely use Java's long type. For each test case we read N, evaluate the expression using 64‑bit arithmetic, and print the result.
solution.timeComplexity: O(1) per test case, O(T) total

code.Java: import java.io.*;import java.util.*;public class Main{public static void main(String[] args) throws Exception{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));StringBuilder sb=new StringBuilder();int T=Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());for(int i=0;i<T;i++){long N=Long.parseLong(br.readLine().trim());long sum=N*(N+1)*(2*N+1)/6;sb.append(sum);if(i<T-1)sb.append('\n');}System.out.print(sb.toString());}}
